original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD): assessment and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, young people, and children with suspected or established NAFLD, with age-specific recommendations.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports metabolic and alcohol history, clinician-reviewed ultrasound, liver and fibrosis results, and follow-up context without diagnosing NAFLD or generating tests, referral, or lifestyle advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.5 — assessment and fibrosis context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports diabetes, metabolic, alcohol, liver-test, ultrasound, and clinician-reviewed fibrosis context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.6–1.2.11 — monitoring and associated conditions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording existing monitoring, cirrhosis context, and associated metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.

title: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD): assessment and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults, young people, and children with suspected or established NAFLD, with age-specific recommendations.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
